project.name: AI Collaboration Protocol
project.purpose: A reusable AI Collaboration Protocol that separates semantic reasoning from implementation while keeping repository metadata synchronized.

externalReferenceModel.principle: ACP repository owns co-ai. Normal project repositories own co-project and reference ACP externally by default.

components.purpose: Explains what ACP is, how to adopt it through external reference, required metadata files, and the generic agent roles.
Reusable ACP protocol files owned by the ACP repository and referenced externally by normal projects.

children.purpose: Defines ACP mission, scope, semantic ownership, roles, startup order, and external-reference principle.
Defines the default ACP working order and an example content workflow.
Defines required project metadata files, ownership, and update responsibilities.
Defines model-independent rules for Semantic Agents.
Defines model-independent rules for Implementation Agents.

components.purpose: Metadata describing ACP itself as an ACP-managed repository.

children.purpose: Documents the current ACP repository architecture and external-reference model.
Provides current orientation for AI collaborators working on ACP.
Machine-readable current state of the ACP repository.
Machine-readable component map for the ACP repository.
Chronological record of ACP repository metadata and structure changes.
Latest report describing metadata synchronization and validation.

components.purpose: Small example README files showing how a co-project folder can look in different repository types.

children.purpose: Intentional example for a physics teaching repository.
Intentional example for an exam visualizer repository.
Intentional example for a game repository.

coreConcepts.label: Repository state and Metadata are the source of truth
Projects reference the ACP repository externally for protocol guidance
Semantic Agent owns semantic reasoning and Knowledge Model design
Implementation Agent owns repository changes and validation
ACP repository co-ai contains reusable protocol rules
Project repositories contain project-specific co-project metadata
